== Notes for [[Item:Q87770|Q87770]] ==

Marcus Fulvius Flaccus was a Roman senator and an ally of the Gracchi.

http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marcus_Fulvius_Flaccus_(consul_125_BC)

SOURCE NOTES:
http://www.american-pictures.com/genealogy/persons/per09753.htm#0

RESEARCH NOTES:
Trib. mil. 180 BCE

Marcus Fulvius Flaccus was a Roman consul in 125 B.C. and a supporter of the Gracchi brothers. Flaccus supported the agrarian commission of Tiberius Gracchus and suggested extending citizenship to Italian landowners. He was forced to abandon his reforms when the Senate sent him to Gaul to help Massilia against an attack by the Saluvii.

Marcus Fulvius Flaccus was consul, but then in 122 he stood for the lower office of tribune. This made Flaccus the junior tribune under Gaius Gracchus. Flaccus was probably the only consul to become tribune after his year as consul.

http://ancienthistory.about.com/library/bl/bl_flaccus.htm

[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
